title: u_printf
text: u_printf Print a formatted message to the Uniface transcript window. Explain this code int u_printf (const char* format {,item {,item} ... }); Copy Parameters format String containing the format, with or without conversion specifications item Zero or more comma-separated arguments, as dictated by the format parameter Return Values Like all members of the printf family, u_printf returns the length of the formatted string. In most cases this value is uninteresting, and you would typically call u_printf as if it were a void function. Use Microsoft Windows platforms only. Library: ulib Description u_printf prints a formatted message to the Uniface transcript window or transcript log file. The syntax of this routine is equivalent to the standard C printf command. A full description of all the formatting options available with printf is outside the scope of this documentation. Consult your C compiler documentation or visit the Microsoft DN documentation at: http://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/docs/ . The following example prints a progress message in the transcript window: Explain this code u_printf("Processing item %d out of %d/n", counter, numitems); Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
